meta.source: Hana's Directive — 2026-02-23 (EXHAUSTIVE rewrite from deep architectural analysis)
meta.maintenance: Update status IMMEDIATELY after every sub-task. Deviations logged within plan. NO priorities. NO estimates. ALL tasks MUST be completed.

globalAlerts: UTMOST PRIORITY: End-to-end PR split workflow via claudemux with REAL integration tests using actual Claude Code
CRITICAL: OllamaProvider (PTY-based, `ollama launch claude`) is the vehicle. NO osm:ollama HTTP module.
CRITICAL: MCP result return channel uses file-based approach — reportClassification/reportSplitPlan write to <result-dir>/<tool>.json
Rule of Two: NO commits until TWO contiguous, issue-free review cycles (diff vs HEAD)
ALL checks MUST pass on ALL platforms (macOS, Linux via Docker, Windows via moo)
Use make make-all-with-log for macOS, make-all-in-container for Linux, make-all-run-windows for Windows
mandatoryDirectives: Read blueprint.json and WIP.md at session start — trust NO chat history
Update blueprint.json status after EVERY sub-task — the next Takumi reads this cold
Aggressive WIP.md checkpointing as token budget tightens
Die coding — work until the very last token
Expand scope continuously — there is ALWAYS more work

statusSection.currentState: IMPLEMENTING — T001-T039+T058-T061 Done. Driving T040+ (coverage push).

sequentialTasks.task: T001: Add MCP result file channel to mcp-instance subcommand
sequentialTasks.description: Add --result-dir flag to osm mcp / mcp-instance. When PR-split MCP tools (reportClassification, reportSplitPlan) are called, write results atomically to <result-dir>/<tool>.json. This is the FUNDAMENTAL architecture for getting structured results from Claude Code back to the orchestrator.

Add reportClassification tool to internal/command/mcp.go. Input: sessionId + files map (path->category). Writes to session event queue AND to result-dir/classification.json if --result-dir set. Input validation: non-empty sessionId, non-empty files map.

Add reportSplitPlan tool to internal/command/mcp.go. Input: sessionId + stages array [{name, files, message, order}]. Writes to session event queue AND to result-dir/split-plan.json. Input validation: non-empty stages, valid stage fields, unique file assignments.

sequentialTasks.task: T004: Write unit tests for reportClassification MCP tool
sequentialTasks.description: Test: valid classification, empty files map, invalid sessionId, result file atomicity, schema validation. Use InMemoryTransport.

sequentialTasks.task: T005: Write unit tests for reportSplitPlan MCP tool
sequentialTasks.description: Test: valid plan, empty stages, duplicate files across stages, invalid stage fields, result file atomicity. Use InMemoryTransport.

sequentialTasks.task: T006: Write unit tests for --result-dir flag and file writing
sequentialTasks.description: Test: atomic write (temp+rename), directory creation, permission errors, file content matches tool input.

sequentialTasks.task: T007: Wire MCPInstanceConfig to include --result-dir in spawn args
sequentialTasks.description: Update MCPInstanceConfig.WriteConfigFile() and SpawnArgs() in internal/builtin/claudemux/mcp_config.go to include --result-dir pointing to the instance state directory.

sequentialTasks.task: T008: Write unit tests for MCPInstanceConfig --result-dir
sequentialTasks.description: Test: generated config includes --result-dir, state directory exists, spawn args correct.

sequentialTasks.task: T009: Implement result file reader utility in claudemux package
sequentialTasks.description: Add ReadClassificationResult(dir) and ReadSplitPlanResult(dir) to claudemux. Read JSON files written by MCP tools. Handle: not found, corrupt JSON, valid parsing.

sequentialTasks.task: T010: Write unit tests for result file reader
sequentialTasks.description: Test: read valid file, file not found, corrupt JSON.
Run make make-all-with-log. All tests pass. Fix any failures.

JS function: get provider from registry, create MCPInstanceConfig, spawn Claude Code with MCP config, send classification prompt via PTY, monitor for completion, read result file, return classification.
JS function: spawn Claude Code, send plan prompt with classification context, wait for reportSplitPlan tool call, read result. Returns ordered stages.

BT node in orchestrate-pr-split.js. Reads files from blackboard, calls classifyChangesWithClaudeMux, writes classification to blackboard.
BT node. Reads classification from blackboard, calls suggestSplitPlanWithClaudeMux, writes plan to blackboard.
Full BT workflow: Analyze -> ClaudeMuxClassify -> ClaudeMuxPlan -> Validate -> Execute -> Verify -> Equivalence.

finalEnforcementProtocol: BOTH wolves fed: Completion AND Perfection
Rule of Two: TWO contiguous, issue-free reviews before ANY commit
If lost: READ DIRECTIVE.txt → THINK → ACT
Die coding — the next Takumi reads WIP.md
